category: Navy General Military Training / Basic Military Requirements
description: Ranks and rates, chain of command, Navy core values, watch standing, damage control basics, Navy history, Sailor's Creed, general orders of a sentry, naval customs and courtesies.

questions.q: What are the three Navy Core Values?
questions.a: Honor, Courage, Commitment.

questions.q: What is the first line of the Sailor's Creed?
questions.a: I am a United States Sailor.

questions.q: In the Sailor's Creed, what does the Sailor represent?
questions.a: The fighting spirit of the Navy and those who have gone before to defend freedom and democracy around the world.

questions.q: How many general orders of a sentry are there?
questions.a: Eleven (11).

questions.q: What is the 1st General Order of a Sentry?
questions.a: To take charge of this post and all government property in view.

questions.q: What is the 2nd General Order of a Sentry?
questions.a: To walk my post in a military manner, keeping always on the alert, and observing everything that takes place within sight or hearing.

questions.q: What is the 3rd General Order of a Sentry?
questions.a: To report all violations of orders I am instructed to enforce.

questions.q: What is the 5th General Order of a Sentry?
questions.a: To quit my post only when properly relieved.

questions.q: What is the 6th General Order of a Sentry?
questions.a: To receive, obey, and pass on to the sentry who relieves me all orders from the commanding officer, command duty officer, officer of the deck, and officers and petty officers of the watch only.

questions.q: What is the 11th General Order of a Sentry?
questions.a: To be especially watchful at night, and during the time for challenging, to challenge all persons on or near my post, and to allow no one to pass without proper authority.

questions.q: What is the lowest enlisted rank in the Navy?
questions.a: Seaman Recruit (E-1).

questions.q: What rank is an E-2 in the Navy?
questions.a: Seaman Apprentice (SA) — or Fireman Apprentice (FA), Constructionman Apprentice (CA), Hospitalman Apprentice (HA), or Airman Apprentice (AA) depending on rating community.

questions.q: What rank is an E-3 in the Navy?
questions.a: Seaman (SN) — or Fireman (FN), Constructionman (CN), Hospitalman (HN), or Airman (AN).

questions.q: At what pay grade does a Sailor first become a Petty Officer?
questions.a: E-4, Petty Officer Third Class (PO3).

questions.q: What rank is an E-5 in the Navy?
questions.a: Petty Officer Second Class (PO2).

questions.q: What rank is an E-6 in the Navy?
questions.a: Petty Officer First Class (PO1).

questions.q: What rank is an E-7 in the Navy?
questions.a: Chief Petty Officer (CPO).

questions.q: What rank is an E-8 in the Navy?
questions.a: Senior Chief Petty Officer (SCPO).

questions.q: What rank is an E-9 in the Navy?
questions.a: Master Chief Petty Officer (MCPO).

questions.q: What is the highest enlisted rank in the Navy?
questions.a: Master Chief Petty Officer of the Navy (MCPON), a special E-9 billet.

questions.q: What is the difference between a rank and a rate in the Navy?
questions.a: Rank is the pay grade (E-1 through E-9). Rate is the combination of pay grade and rating (job specialty), e.g., BM2 (Boatswain's Mate Second Class).

questions.q: What is a 'rating' in the Navy?
questions.a: An enlisted occupational specialty, indicated by a specific badge and abbreviation (e.g., IT for Information Systems Technician, HM for Hospital Corpsman).

questions.q: What color is the Navy working uniform (NWU) Type III?
questions.a: Woodland green camouflage pattern (AOR-2).

questions.q: What is the Navy dress white uniform called?
questions.a: Dress Whites (Service Dress White or Full Dress White depending on the occasion).

questions.q: What does OOD stand for aboard a Navy vessel?
questions.a: Officer of the Deck — the officer on watch responsible to the commanding officer for the safe operation of the ship.

questions.q: What is the purpose of watch standing in the Navy?
questions.a: To maintain the security, safety, and operational readiness of the ship or installation 24 hours a day.

questions.q: What are the three standard watch rotations aboard ship?
questions.a: Port and Starboard (two sections), three-section, and four-section watches, typically in 4-hour shifts (e.g., 0000-0400, 0400-0800).

questions.q: What is the 'quarterdeck'?
questions.a: The designated area aboard a ship or at a shore command that serves as the ceremonial and watch station entry/exit point.

questions.q: What is Damage Control (DC)?
questions.a: The measures taken aboard a ship to prevent, contain, and repair damage from battle, fire, flooding, or other emergencies.

questions.q: What are the three elements of the DC 'fire triangle'?
questions.a: Heat, Fuel, and Oxygen.

questions.q: What are the classes of fire in the Navy?
questions.a: Alpha (ordinary combustibles), Bravo (flammable liquids), Charlie (electrical), and Delta (combustible metals).

questions.q: What type of fire extinguisher is used on a Class Bravo fire?
questions.a: AFFF (Aqueous Film Forming Foam) or PKP (Purple-K Powder/Potassium Bicarbonate).

questions.q: What does 'AFFF' stand for?
questions.a: Aqueous Film Forming Foam.

questions.q: What is the primary method for stopping flooding aboard ship?
questions.a: Shoring — using wooden or metal braces to reinforce and seal damaged bulkheads and hull breaches.

questions.q: What color identifies a fire station aboard a Navy ship?
questions.a: Red.

questions.q: What condition of readiness means 'maximum watertight integrity' (battle stations)?
questions.a: Condition Zebra.

questions.q: What condition of readiness is set during normal peacetime cruising?
questions.a: Condition Yoke (modified watertight integrity).

questions.q: What date is the U.S. Navy's birthday?
questions.a: October 13, 1775.

questions.q: What was the first ship of the Continental Navy?
questions.a: USS Alfred (originally the merchant ship Black Prince, converted in 1775).

questions.q: Who is known as the 'Father of the American Navy'?
questions.a: John Paul Jones.

questions.q: What famous phrase is attributed to John Paul Jones?
questions.a: 'I have not yet begun to fight!'

questions.q: What battle in 1898 saw Commodore Dewey defeat the Spanish fleet in the Philippines?
questions.a: The Battle of Manila Bay.

questions.q: What was the significance of the Battle of Midway (June 1942)?
questions.a: It was the turning point of the Pacific War in WWII. The U.S. Navy sank four Japanese carriers.

questions.q: When rendering honors to the national ensign during morning or evening colors, what should a Sailor in uniform do outdoors?
questions.a: Stop, face the flag (or the music if the flag is not visible), and render a hand salute.

questions.q: On a Navy ship, what is the proper way to request permission to come aboard?
questions.a: Face the national ensign and salute, then salute the OOD and state 'Request permission to come aboard, Sir/Ma'am.'

questions.q: On a Navy ship, what is the proper way to request permission to go ashore?
questions.a: Salute the OOD and state 'Request permission to go ashore, Sir/Ma'am,' then face the national ensign and salute upon departing.

questions.q: What does 'aye aye' mean in the Navy?
questions.a: 'I understand and I will obey.' It is the proper response to an order from a superior.

questions.q: What is a 'Plan of the Day' (POD)?
questions.a: The official daily schedule published by the command detailing the day's activities, uniform requirements, and other information.

questions.q: What does NAVPERS stand for?
questions.a: Naval Personnel — the bureau that manages Navy personnel policies and programs (Bureau of Naval Personnel, BUPERS).

questions.q: What is the primary reference for basic military requirements for enlisted Sailors?
questions.a: NAVEDTRA 14325, Basic Military Requirements (BMR).
